user_input: 我想研究小样本时间序列异常检测，附了一篇 Anomaly Transformer，可以以它为基线继续改吗？

supplied_materials.title: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

clarification_questions: 时间序列是单变量还是多变量，异常标签大约有多少？
需要在线流式检测，还是离线批量分析？

candidate_families: Patch-based or state-space baseline when appropriate
baseline_expectation.preferred_role: The supplied paper must be verified and compared with simpler reconstruction/forecasting baselines before freezing it.

parallel_expectations.mechanism: use unlabeled normal data and scarce anomaly labels

hypothesis: If labels are scarce, directly fine-tuning the association discrepancy objective is unstable; self-supervised normal-pattern learning plus calibrated event-level scoring should improve low-label detection without exploiting test labels.

alternatives: use the supplied paper as a parallel method if reproduction is unreliable
choose an online detector when streaming is required
tailoring_advice.recommended_stitch: Do not automatically accept the supplied paper as baseline. Reproduce or audit it, compare to simple baselines, then add one low-label adaptation module behind a switch.
reject: using point adjustment that inflates scores without disclosure
calibrating threshold on the test set

stop_conditions: No-go as baseline if it cannot be reproduced within budget.
Stop if gains rely on test-set thresholding.
Revise if few-shot labels are not representative.

required: distinguish verified, inferred, proposed, and unknown statements
verify supplied-paper identity and role before using it
reject irrelevant retrieval results even when DOI or metadata is valid
bind every core paper to a specific gap, module, comparison, or risk role
produce a minimal pilot before a full combined method
forbidden: fabricate paper identifiers, results, code availability, or reproduced metrics
treat keyword overlap as sufficient evidence
force supplied papers to be compatible
claim novelty from A+B+C composition alone
hide stronger baselines, failed modules, or negative results

user_input: 我上传了 MobileNetV3 论文，想把它用在轻量化植物病害识别上
clarification_questions: 数据是实验室背景还是田间自然场景？
准备部署在手机、树莓派还是其他设备？

baseline_expectation.preferred_role: The supplied MobileNetV3 paper is a backbone/baseline candidate, not itself a plant-disease contribution.

parallel_expectations.mechanism: reduce edge cost after accuracy baseline is established

hypothesis: In field imagery, small symptom regions and background shift limit a plain lightweight backbone; one symptom-focused multi-scale or crop-aware mechanism should improve macro-F1 while preserving mobile latency.

tailoring_advice.recommended_stitch: Freeze MobileNetV3 as a baseline/backbone, diagnose field errors, then add either a lightweight symptom-localization mechanism or imbalance objective; compress only after selecting the best architecture.
reject: claiming novelty from using MobileNetV3 in a new dataset

experiment_plan: macro-F1, calibration, device latency, model size
stop_conditions: Stop if lab-to-field shift dominates architecture.
Revise if disease labels are visually ambiguous.
No-go for mobile claim without device measurement.

required: distinguish verified, inferred, proposed, and unknown statements
verify supplied-paper identity and role before using it
reject irrelevant retrieval results even when DOI or metadata is valid
bind every core paper to a specific gap, module, comparison, or risk role
produce a minimal pilot before a full combined method
forbidden: fabricate paper identifiers, results, code availability, or reproduced metrics
treat keyword overlap as sufficient evidence
force supplied papers to be compatible
claim novelty from A+B+C composition alone
hide stronger baselines, failed modules, or negative results

user_input: 想做无人机小目标检测，我提供了 YOLOX 和 TinyDet 两篇论文，看看能不能把它们的思路结合起来

supplied_materials.title: TinyDet: Accurate Small Object Detection in Lightweight Generic Detectors

clarification_questions: 希望从 YOLOX-Tiny/YOLOX-S 哪个规模开始，还是由系统根据算力推荐？
主要目标是提高 AP_small，还是还需要边缘实时部署？

baseline_expectation.preferred_role: Verify YOLOX variant as baseline and reproduce it before integration.

parallel_expectations.mechanism: retain shallow/high-resolution evidence while controlling compute

candidate_families: YOLOX with P2/high-resolution lightweight neck papers
parallel_expectations.mechanism: check whether the exact stitch already exists

hypothesis: YOLOX loses tiny-target evidence at coarse feature levels; a carefully adapted TinyDet-style high-resolution lightweight branch may improve AP_small, but only if feature semantics and compute remain compatible.

alternatives: treat TinyDet as a comparison if its mechanism cannot be cleanly separated
use P2-only adaptation as a simpler pilot
tailoring_advice.recommended_stitch: Map TinyDet components to YOLOX feature stages, choose one transferable component, and implement it behind a switch; do not transplant the entire architecture.
reject: assuming compatibility from matching tensor shapes
calling YOLOX+TinyDet novel before near-duplicate search

experiment_plan: E2 lightweight operator only if E1 cost is excessive
AP_small, total AP, params, FLOPs, memory and target-device latency
stop_conditions: No-go if YOLOX baseline is not reproducible.
Stop if the added path duplicates existing YOLOX-P2 work without a distinct hypothesis.
Revise if gains come only from higher input resolution.

required: distinguish verified, inferred, proposed, and unknown statements
verify supplied-paper identity and role before using it
reject irrelevant retrieval results even when DOI or metadata is valid
bind every core paper to a specific gap, module, comparison, or risk role
produce a minimal pilot before a full combined method
forbidden: fabricate paper identifiers, results, code availability, or reproduced metrics
treat keyword overlap as sufficient evidence
force supplied papers to be compatible
claim novelty from A+B+C composition alone
hide stronger baselines, failed modules, or negative results

user_input: 我上传了 U-Net 论文，想做医学图像分割，但还没有确定具体器官和数据集

supplied_materials.title: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation

clarification_questions: 你更容易获得哪类数据：CT、MRI、超声、病理或显微图像？
希望做二维切片分割，还是三维体数据分割？

candidate_families: nnU-Net as a strong auto-configuring comparison
baseline_expectation.preferred_role: U-Net is only a provisional baseline until modality and dimensionality are fixed.

parallel_expectations.mechanism: selected only after dataset error analysis

hypothesis: No method hypothesis should be frozen before the organ, modality, dimensionality, and dominant segmentation failure are known.

alternatives: 3D project when inter-slice context is essential
tailoring_advice.recommended_stitch: Do not fabricate a concrete U-Net modification. First shortlist feasible public datasets, select modality/dimensionality, run U-Net and nnU-Net-like baselines, then derive one evidence-backed module from error analysis.
reject: immediately proposing Attention U-Net without a task gap
using the uploaded paper as proof that U-Net is still the strongest baseline

stop_conditions: Current decision remains REVISE until task/data are fixed.
No-go if data license or annotation access is unavailable.
Stop clinical claims without external validation.

required: distinguish verified, inferred, proposed, and unknown statements
verify supplied-paper identity and role before using it
reject irrelevant retrieval results even when DOI or metadata is valid
bind every core paper to a specific gap, module, comparison, or risk role
produce a minimal pilot before a full combined method
forbidden: fabricate paper identifiers, results, code availability, or reproduced metrics
treat keyword overlap as sufficient evidence
force supplied papers to be compatible
claim novelty from A+B+C composition alone
hide stronger baselines, failed modules, or negative results

user_input: 想优化推荐系统，我带了 LightGCN 作为基线，还上传了一篇关于对比学习推荐的论文，帮我看看怎么组合比较合理

supplied_materials.title: LightGCN: Simplifying and Powering Graph Convolution Network for Recommendation

clarification_questions: 请确认对比学习论文的标题或 DOI，便于判断它属于图增强、噪声扰动还是多视图对比。
你的数据是点击/购买等隐式反馈吗，主要问题是稀疏性还是冷启动？

baseline_expectation.preferred_role: Verify and reproduce LightGCN with the dataset-specific split and negative sampling.

candidate_families: noise-based contrastive such as embedding perturbation
parallel_expectations.mechanism: must be identified from the actual paper before transfer
separate contrastive benefit from stronger regularization and tuning

hypothesis: In sparse implicit-feedback graphs, contrastive regularization may stabilize representations; the chosen augmentation must preserve recommendation semantics and outperform matched regularization under the same tuning budget.

alternatives: use SimGCL-like perturbation when graph augmentations destroy sparse edges
skip contrastive learning if sparsity is not the observed failure
tailoring_advice.recommended_stitch: Verify the exact parallel paper, reproduce LightGCN, implement only its core contrastive term/view generator behind a switch, and compare against simple noise/regularization controls.
reject: using extra views and larger training budget without control

experiment_plan: Recall/NDCG, training cost, stability across seeds
stop_conditions: Block method design until the supplied paper identity is verified.
Stop if gains vanish under matched tuning.
Revise if augmentations violate temporal or interaction semantics.

required: distinguish verified, inferred, proposed, and unknown statements
verify supplied-paper identity and role before using it
reject irrelevant retrieval results even when DOI or metadata is valid
bind every core paper to a specific gap, module, comparison, or risk role
produce a minimal pilot before a full combined method
forbidden: fabricate paper identifiers, results, code availability, or reproduced metrics
treat keyword overlap as sufficient evidence
force supplied papers to be compatible
claim novelty from A+B+C composition alone
hide stronger baselines, failed modules, or negative results
